requirements.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list
Notes are displayed with their titles visible

User can access a search function
Search input field is visible or accessible
User can access an AI chat or assistant feature
Chat interface has an input field for questions

New note creation is accessible from the main screen
Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note
Formatting toolbar or options are available

App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)
Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile
Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: Screenshot shows a list of notes on a mobile device, which is a hierarchical structure as they are nested in categories and subcategories. This indicates that the user can view their notes in a categorized manner, aligning with the requirement.
results.notes: The screenshot does not provide enough detail to assess if all notes are visible or if there are any other aspects related to the requirement. However, it is clear that the user has access to different categories and subcategories of notes.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there are multiple notes visible with their titles in the notes section of an app. The notes are clearly labeled and easy to read.
results.notes: The user interface seems well-organized, but it's important to ensure that all the functionality related to notes works as expected. This includes editing, deleting, and filtering notes.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: There is no visible button or option for creating a new note in this screenshot.
results.notes: The image appears to be a phone screen displaying a messaging app with various message threads. The UI does not contain any elements that are immediately recognizable as being related to the requirement of having a 'Create Note' button or option.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a '+' button in the top right corner of the app interface which serves as an indicator for new note creation. The main screen includes the option to create a new note.
results.notes: The '+' button is easily accessible and stands out as an inviting actionable element on the main screen. This implies that users can quickly access the note creation feature from the main interface, fulfilling the requirement.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: No nesting or hierarchy is visible on the screen based on the given image. There are no indications of a nested list or a structured hierarchical layout.
results.notes: From this screenshot, it is not possible to determine if there are any features in the app that support nesting or hierarchy as it appears to be a standard iOS keyboard view with no context or specific UI elements related to the requirement. To accurately assess this requirement, a different screen or state of the app would need to be examined.

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a list of notes on an app with a '+' button to add more notes at the bottom and some notes have been expanded to show sub-notes. The indentation is visible for these nested notes, but it's not clear if this is part of the default view or a result of expanding a note. Additionally, there are two icons on the top right corner that look like they might be used for sorting or filtering the notes list. It's unclear if this screenshot captures all features and states of the app.
results.notes: From what is visible in the screenshot, it seems that at least part of the requirement regarding indentation or folder structure being visible for nested notes is met. However, without more context or a different screen/state of the app, it's not possible to definitively say whether all aspects of the requirement are met.

results.notes: It appears that the editor area may be hidden behind other UI elements, or it could be located elsewhere within the app that isn't visible in this screenshot. It would be necessary to test different states of the app to ensure this requirement is met.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

results.evidence: In the screenshot provided, it is evident that the user can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists). The image shows multiple instances of text blocks that include different types of content, such as paragraphs and bullet points. These blocks are clearly separated by different levels of indents or formatting styles, making it easy for users to distinguish between them.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a smartphone displaying an app interface. The navigation bar at the bottom of the screen contains three tabs or buttons for navigating through different sections of the app.
results.notes: Please note that the full functionality and mobile-appropriateness of the navigation can only be assessed when interacting with the app, not just by looking at a static screenshot. It is also important to consider factors such as touch targets and ease of use in addition to the presence of bottom tabs or hamburger menus.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: In the screenshot, we can see a cell phone keyboard and a message screen. The touch targets, such as buttons on the keyboard, are not visible in this image to evaluate their appropriateness for mobile users.
results.notes: More information about the content of the message screen is needed to determine if any other UI elements are present that could be related to the requirement.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.evidence: Content within the screenshot appears to be partially cut off or obscured by other UI elements, such as the keyboard at the bottom of the screen, making it difficult to read the entire content without horizontal scrolling.
results.notes: It is not possible to assess the entire layout and content due to the presence of non-textual UI elements like the keyboard obstructing part of the content. The app should consider adjusting the keyboard placement or redesigning the layout for better readability.
